item.title: about
item.description: brief biography 🍮 I was born in San Jose, California and grew up in Gilbert, Arizona. I did my undergrad at Arizona State University and graduated with two degrees in Physics and Astrophysics. Afterwords, I went to Ohio State University and got my PhD in Physics in December 2023. My PhD was focused on understanding the physics and phenomonology of multi-phase galactic winds from starburst galaxies.Near the end of my PhD, I did a summer internship at Los Alamos National Laboratory, where I began my transition to Scientific Machine Learning and researched Universal Differential Equations applied to classic non-linear ODEs.

item.title: contact
item.description: contact information ✉️ I can be reached via email: dnguyen.phys@gmail.com or LinkedIn messaging: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dustinnguyen24/

item.title: research
item.description: Scientific Machine Learning In my spare time I am independently conducting research on NCDEs and UDEs. 🌀 The most up-to-date list of my publications can be found on ADS or arXiv. I have not published since my PhD (2018-2023). I will update this page when I push something new out! List of SciML related publications (+ denotes co-authors): Nguyen, ICML 2023 Workshop on Machine Learning for Astrophysics Nguyen+, NeurIPS 2023 Workshop on Machine Learning and the Physical Sciences 🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️🎧🪴💻☕️
